slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 3 - Research Methodology
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'starts with a general theory or hypothesis and tests it through specific empirical observations.'?
options: Deductive Research Approach
Empirical Research
Action Research
Cross-Sectional Study
slides.explanation: Correct! **Deductive Research Approach** is defined as: starts with a general theory or hypothesis and tests it through specific empirical observations. (top-down theory testing).

slides.question: Q2: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'starts with specific observations or measurements and moves toward broader generalizations and theories.'?
options: Quantitative Research
Case Study Method
Theoretical Research
Inductive Research Approach
slides.explanation: Correct! **Inductive Research Approach** is defined as: starts with specific observations or measurements and moves toward broader generalizations and theories. (bottom-up theory construction).

slides.question: Q3: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a qualitative approach that focuses on describing the lived experiences of individuals regarding a specific concept or phenomenon.'?
options: Analytical Research
Experimental Research
Qualitative Research
Phenomenological Research
slides.explanation: Correct! **Phenomenological Research** is defined as: is a qualitative approach that focuses on describing the lived experiences of individuals regarding a specific concept or phenomenon. (lived experience description).

slides.question: Q4: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a systematic qualitative methodology that involves the generation of theory from systematic analysis of data.'?
options: Conceptual Research
Descriptive Research
Grounded Theory
Diagnostic Research
slides.explanation: Correct! **Grounded Theory** is defined as: is a systematic qualitative methodology that involves the generation of theory from systematic analysis of data. (data-driven theory generation).

slides.question: Q5: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'involves the immersive study of people and cultures in their natural environment over an extended period.'?
options: Exploratory Research
Ethnographic Research
Ex-Post Facto Research
Evaluative Research
slides.explanation: Correct! **Ethnographic Research** is defined as: involves the immersive study of people and cultures in their natural environment over an extended period. (immersive cultural study).

slides.question: Q6: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Fundamental Research**?
options: Its primary focus is long-term repeated observation.
Its primary focus is basic / pure research.
Its primary focus is similarity and difference analysis.
Its primary focus is past event analysis.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Fundamental Research** is: **basic / pure research**.

slides.question: Q7: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Applied Research**?
options: Its primary focus is empirical observation and evidence.
Its primary focus is practical problem-solving.
Its primary focus is top-down theory testing.
Its primary focus is single point-in-time snapshot.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Applied Research** is: **practical problem-solving**.

slides.question: Q8: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Action Research**?
options: Its primary focus is bottom-up theory construction.
Its primary focus is abstract model development.
Its primary focus is in-depth single unit analysis.
Its primary focus is immediate local action.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Action Research** is: **immediate local action**.

slides.question: Q9: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Quantitative Research**?
options: Its primary focus is cause-and-effect verification.
Its primary focus is critical factual evaluation.
Its primary focus is lived experience description.
Its primary focus is numerical and statistical analysis.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Quantitative Research** is: **numerical and statistical analysis**.

slides.question: Q10: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Qualitative Research**?
options: Its primary focus is non-numerical thematic analysis.
Its primary focus is cause-and-association diagnosis.
Its primary focus is abstract concept re-interpretation.
Its primary focus is data-driven theory generation.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Qualitative Research** is: **non-numerical thematic analysis**.

slides.question: Q11: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the probability that a statistical test will correctly reject a false null hypothesis, equal to 1 minus beta.'?
options: Extraneous Variable
Pilot Study
Null Hypothesis (H0)
Statistical Power
slides.explanation: Correct! **Statistical Power** is defined as: is the probability that a statistical test will correctly reject a false null hypothesis, equal to 1 minus beta. (correct rejection probability (1-beta)).

slides.question: Q12: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'specifies the expected direction of the relationship or difference between variables (e.g., group A will score higher than B).'?
options: Confounding Variable
Exploratory Design
Directional Hypothesis
Alternative Hypothesis (H1)
slides.explanation: Correct! **Directional Hypothesis** is defined as: specifies the expected direction of the relationship or difference between variables (e.g., group A will score higher than B). (expected relationship direction statement).

slides.question: Q13: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'predicts that there is a relationship or difference, but does not specify which group will be higher or lower.'?
options: Descriptive Design
Type I Error
Control Variable
Non-Directional Hypothesis
slides.explanation: Correct! **Non-Directional Hypothesis** is defined as: predicts that there is a relationship or difference, but does not specify which group will be higher or lower. (direction-free relationship statement).

slides.question: Q14: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the extent to which a study establishes a trustworthy cause-and-effect relationship, free from confounding variables.'?
options: Type II Error
Control Group
Internal Validity
Literature Review
slides.explanation: Correct! **Internal Validity** is defined as: is the extent to which a study establishes a trustworthy cause-and-effect relationship, free from confounding variables. (confound-free cause-effect trust).

slides.question: Q15: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the extent to which the results of a study can be generalized to other situations, people, and times.'?
options: Research Problem
Quasi-Experimental Design
Independent Variable
External Validity
slides.explanation: Correct! **External Validity** is defined as: is the extent to which the results of a study can be generalized to other situations, people, and times. (results generalization capability).

slides.question: Q16: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Research Design**?
options: Its primary focus is measured outcome variable.
Its primary focus is conceptual schema explanation.
Its primary focus is participant-researcher bias prevention.
Its primary focus is framework for data collection and analysis.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Research Design** is: **framework for data collection and analysis**.

slides.question: Q17: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Research Hypothesis**?
options: Its primary focus is feasibility and scale trial.
Its primary focus is correct rejection probability (1-beta).
Its primary focus is testable tentative assumption.
Its primary focus is uncontrolled confounding variable.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Research Hypothesis** is: **testable tentative assumption**.

slides.question: Q18: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Null Hypothesis (H0)**?
options: Its primary focus is insight and familiarity collection.
Its primary focus is no-effect assumption.
Its primary focus is expected relationship direction statement.
Its primary focus is relationship-masking variable.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Null Hypothesis (H0)** is: **no-effect assumption**.

slides.question: Q19: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Alternative Hypothesis (H1)**?
options: Its primary focus is accurate characteristic profiling.
Its primary focus is expected effect statement.
Its primary focus is constant baseline variable.
Its primary focus is direction-free relationship statement.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Alternative Hypothesis (H1)** is: **expected effect statement**.

slides.question: Q20: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Type I Error**?
options: Its primary focus is critical gap analysis of past works.
Its primary focus is untreated baseline group.
Its primary focus is false positive (rejecting true H0).
Its primary focus is confound-free cause-effect trust.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Type I Error** is: **false positive (rejecting true H0)**.

slides.question: Q21: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a qualitative technique where a small group of people (usually 6-10) discuss a topic guided by a moderator.'?
options: Focus Group Discussion
Secondary Data
Quota Sampling
Stratified Random Sampling
slides.explanation: Correct! **Focus Group Discussion** is defined as: is a qualitative technique where a small group of people (usually 6-10) discuss a topic guided by a moderator. (moderated group discussion).

slides.question: Q22: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a research tool used to determine the presence of certain words, themes, or concepts within qualitative data.'?
options: Systematic Sampling
Content Analysis
Questionnaire
Purposive Sampling
slides.explanation: Correct! **Content Analysis** is defined as: is a research tool used to determine the presence of certain words, themes, or concepts within qualitative data. (qualitative data theme analysis).

slides.question: Q23: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the use of multiple data sources or collection methods to study a single phenomenon to increase validity.'?
options: Snowball Sampling
Cluster Sampling
Data Triangulation
Structured Interview
slides.explanation: Correct! **Data Triangulation** is defined as: is the use of multiple data sources or collection methods to study a single phenomenon to increase validity. (multi-source validation technique).

slides.question: Q24: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the percentage of people who completed the research instrument out of the total sample selected.'?
options: Response Rate
Likert Scale
Sampling Frame Error
Multi-Stage Sampling
slides.explanation: Correct! **Response Rate** is defined as: is the percentage of people who completed the research instrument out of the total sample selected. (completion ratio of sample).

slides.question: Q25: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the trial administration of a questionnaire to a small group to detect and fix ambiguities before main collection.'?
options: Sampling Bias
Non-Probability Sampling
Pilot Testing
Semantic Differential Scale
slides.explanation: Correct! **Pilot Testing** is defined as: is the trial administration of a questionnaire to a small group to detect and fix ambiguities before main collection. (questionnaire design validation).

slides.question: Q26: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Probability Sampling**?
options: Its primary focus is systematic behavior recording.
Its primary focus is original direct collection.
Its primary focus is unbiased generalization potential.
Its primary focus is accessibility-based collection.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Probability Sampling** is: **unbiased generalization potential**.

slides.question: Q27: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Simple Random Sampling**?
options: Its primary focus is moderated group discussion.
Its primary focus is lottery or random number selection.
Its primary focus is previously collected material.
Its primary focus is non-random strata filling.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Simple Random Sampling** is: **lottery or random number selection**.

slides.question: Q28: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Stratified Random Sampling**?
options: Its primary focus is standardized self-report tool.
Its primary focus is qualitative data theme analysis.
Its primary focus is ensuring representation of subgroups.
Its primary focus is judgmental target selection.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Stratified Random Sampling** is: **ensuring representation of subgroups**.

slides.question: Q29: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Systematic Sampling**?
options: Its primary focus is multi-source validation technique.
Its primary focus is referral-based chain network.
Its primary focus is rigid standardized interview.
Its primary focus is fixed interval sampling.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Systematic Sampling** is: **fixed interval sampling**.

slides.question: Q30: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Cluster Sampling**?
options: Its primary focus is geographical or group-level division.
Its primary focus is measuring ordinal/interval agreement levels.
Its primary focus is completion ratio of sample.
Its primary focus is frame-population mismatch error.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Cluster Sampling** is: **geographical or group-level division**.

slides.question: Q31: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a distribution with a tail extending toward the left side (lower values), where Mean < Median < Mode.'?
options: Negatively Skewed
Level of Significance
Parametric Test
Mode
slides.explanation: Correct! **Negatively Skewed** is defined as: is a distribution with a tail extending toward the left side (lower values), where Mean < Median < Mode. (left-tail distribution skew).

slides.question: Q32: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'measures the tail-heaviness or peakedness of a probability distribution relative to a normal distribution.'?
options: Kurtosis
Standard Deviation
Non-Parametric Test
Confidence Interval
slides.explanation: Correct! **Kurtosis** is defined as: measures the tail-heaviness or peakedness of a probability distribution relative to a normal distribution. (peakedness or flatness score).

slides.question: Q33: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a non-parametric alternative to the independent t-test used to compare two independent groups.'?
options: Variance
Chi-Square Test
Mann-Whitney U Test
Degrees of Freedom
slides.explanation: Correct! **Mann-Whitney U Test** is defined as: is a non-parametric alternative to the independent t-test used to compare two independent groups. (distribution-free two-group comparison).

slides.question: Q34: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a non-parametric alternative to the paired t-test used to compare two related samples.'?
options: Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test
ANOVA (Analysis of Variance)
Pearson Correlation
Standard Error of the Mean
slides.explanation: Correct! **Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test** is defined as: is a non-parametric alternative to the paired t-test used to compare two related samples. (distribution-free paired comparison).

slides.question: Q35: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is a non-parametric alternative to one-way ANOVA used to compare three or more independent groups.'?
options: Student's t-Test
Kruskal-Wallis Test
Spearman Rank Correlation
Normal Distribution
slides.explanation: Correct! **Kruskal-Wallis Test** is defined as: is a non-parametric alternative to one-way ANOVA used to compare three or more independent groups. (distribution-free multi-group comparison).

slides.question: Q36: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Arithmetic Mean**?
options: Its primary focus is large-sample mean comparison.
Its primary focus is slope relationship prediction.
Its primary focus is right-tail distribution skew.
Its primary focus is arithmetic average calculation.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Arithmetic Mean** is: **arithmetic average calculation**.

slides.question: Q37: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Median**?
options: Its primary focus is left-tail distribution skew.
Its primary focus is middle positional indicator.
Its primary focus is alpha threshold for hypothesis rejection.
Its primary focus is distribution-assumed statistical testing.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Median** is: **middle positional indicator**.

slides.question: Q38: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Mode**?
options: Its primary focus is parameter estimation range.
Its primary focus is peakedness or flatness score.
Its primary focus is highest frequency value.
Its primary focus is distribution-free statistical testing.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Mode** is: **highest frequency value**.

slides.question: Q39: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Standard Deviation**?
options: Its primary focus is categorical association verification.
Its primary focus is independent observation count limit.
Its primary focus is dispersion measurement tool.
Its primary focus is distribution-free two-group comparison.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Standard Deviation** is: **dispersion measurement tool**.

slides.question: Q40: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Variance**?
options: Its primary focus is comparing three or more group means.
Its primary focus is squared dispersion measure.
Its primary focus is distribution-free paired comparison.
Its primary focus is sample mean precision estimate.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Variance** is: **squared dispersion measure**.

slides.question: Q41: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the unethical practice of inventing data or results and recording or reporting them as true research outcomes.'?
options: Bibliography
APA Style
Impact Factor
Fabrication
slides.explanation: Correct! **Fabrication** is defined as: is the unethical practice of inventing data or results and recording or reporting them as true research outcomes. (inventing non-existent research data).

slides.question: Q42: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the unethical practice of manipulating research materials, equipment, or processes, or changing or omitting data.'?
options: Falsification
H-Index
MLA Style
Abstract
slides.explanation: Correct! **Falsification** is defined as: is the unethical practice of manipulating research materials, equipment, or processes, or changing or omitting data. (manipulating active research data).

slides.question: Q43: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'occurs when an investigator's private interests or relationships compromise the professional judgment of research.'?
options: Conflict of Interest
Chicago Style
Footnote
Institutional Review Board (IRB)
slides.explanation: Correct! **Conflict of Interest** is defined as: occurs when an investigator's private interests or relationships compromise the professional judgment of research. (private interest compromising professional work).

slides.question: Q44: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the portion of a research paper dedicated to thanking individuals, funders, or institutions who aided the study.'?
options: Research Proposal
Intellectual Property Rights (IPR)
Acknowledgment Section
Informed Consent
slides.explanation: Correct! **Acknowledgment Section** is defined as: is the portion of a research paper dedicated to thanking individuals, funders, or institutions who aided the study. (thanking contributors and funders section).

slides.question: Q45: Which of the following research terms is defined as: 'is the standardized sequence of chapters (Introduction, Literature, Methods, Results, Discussion) for reporting research.'?
options: Peer Review
Patent
Research Monograph
Thesis Layout Structure
slides.explanation: Correct! **Thesis Layout Structure** is defined as: is the standardized sequence of chapters (Introduction, Literature, Methods, Results, Discussion) for reporting research. (standardized academic chapter sequence).

slides.question: Q46: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Plagiarism**?
options: Its primary focus is tracking scientific citations system.
Its primary focus is protection of expression of ideas.
Its primary focus is barrier-free research accessibility.
Its primary focus is academic theft and misattribution.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Plagiarism** is: **academic theft and misattribution**.

slides.question: Q47: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Self-Plagiarism**?
options: Its primary focus is own-work recycling without citation.
Its primary focus is inventing non-existent research data.
Its primary focus is consulted sources comprehensive list.
Its primary focus is journal citation frequency index.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Self-Plagiarism** is: **own-work recycling without citation**.

slides.question: Q48: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **APA Style**?
options: Its primary focus is author-date citation style.
Its primary focus is manipulating active research data.
Its primary focus is brief paper summary overview.
Its primary focus is citation and publication volume balance metric.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **APA Style** is: **author-date citation style**.

slides.question: Q49: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **MLA Style**?
options: Its primary focus is bottom-of-page contextual note.
Its primary focus is private interest compromising professional work.
Its primary focus is author-page citation style.
Its primary focus is human subject safety oversight board.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **MLA Style** is: **author-page citation style**.

slides.question: Q50: In research methodology, what is the primary focus, role, or application of **Chicago Style**?
options: Its primary focus is thanking contributors and funders section.
Its primary focus is notes-bibliography citation formatting.
Its primary focus is planned research blueprint outline.
Its primary focus is voluntary participant agreement process.
slides.explanation: Correct! The primary focus of **Chicago Style** is: **notes-bibliography citation formatting**.

slides.title: Discussion & Review 💬
slides.content: Review your answers for Mock Exam 3. Pay special attention to topics where you selected incorrect answers!

box.content: You have completed Research Methodology Mock Exam 3.
box.note: Solve all 10 mock exams to master the 500 Research Methodology questions!
